evidence: Event-channel pattern (mpsc + EventLoopProxy wakeup) is clean and consistently used across all cross-thread boundaries.
load_thumbnails_for_dir clones the full file list before applying the take() cap.
Slideshow next-time check reads slideshow_next_time twice in the same event polling loop.

issues.summary: load_thumbnails_for_dir clones all file paths then takes only the first MAX_THUMBNAILS
evidence: Line 98: `let files: Vec<PathBuf> = self.ui_state.files.iter().map(|f| f.path.clone()).collect()` — unconditional full clone.
Line 110: `files.into_iter().take(n)` — only n clones are ever used.

issues.summary: slideshow_next_time is read twice in successive if-blocks in process_events
evidence: Two separate if-let blocks reading slideshow_next_time within the same event loop iteration — one for advancing the slide, one for updating next wake time.
These two reads could be unified into a single helper that both advances and records next_time.
issues.suggestion: Combine the two slideshow_next_time reads into `if let Some(t) = self.slideshow_next_time { if now >= t { self.next_image(); self.slideshow_next_time = Some(now + self.slideshow_interval); wait_until = Some(now + self.slideshow_interval); } }`

root_causes: Incrementally added fields without revisiting allocation patterns.
likely_symptoms: Unnecessary allocation pressure on directories with many images; subtle slideshow timing drift.
